description: Interactive drag-the-magnet sorting game. One everyday object sits on the table at a time; the student drags (or taps) a single horseshoe magnet onto it to test it. Iron and steel objects leap up and cling to the poles; everything else only wobbles and stays put. Each tested object drops into a Magnetic or Non-magnetic tray, and once all objects are tested the game shows a two-column Magnetic vs Non-magnetic results table with a replay button.
curriculum: NCERT Grade 6 · Science · Magnetic and Non-magnetic Materials

width.description: Accepted by the prop signature but advisory only — the component is fully responsive and measures its own container via ResizeObserver, so it adapts to whatever width it is given.

height.description: Accepted by the prop signature but advisory only — the play board uses a responsive clamped height and the component lays itself out from its measured size.

gradeLevel.description: Tunes the celebration tier on the results screen: confetti amount, the size of the 'Sorted!' heading, and the replay button wording (6 = 'Play again!', most playful; 7 = 'Play again'; 8 = 'Try again', most restrained). May also be supplied inside data.gradeLevel; the top-level prop wins.

data.description: Tool configuration object. Any subset is merged over the built-in defaults via {...DEFAULT_CONFIG, ...data}. This is the primary way an agent customises the tool — see the 'data' schema below.
Configuration merged over the defaults. Pass only the fields you want to change.

title.description: Main heading shown in the gradient header.

subtitle.default: Test each object with the magnet — which ones cling?

instruction.default: Drag the magnet down onto each object. Magnetic ones leap up and cling to the poles!
instruction.description: Instruction line shown above the play board.

onScreenCount.description: Number of objects presented at once. The current component always presents objects one at a time; this field is reserved and does not yet change that behaviour.

gradeLevel.description: Celebration tier (see parameters.gradeLevel). The top-level gradeLevel prop, if provided, overrides this.

objects.description: The pool of objects the student tests. The component reshuffles this into a well-mixed order at the start of every game (it avoids long runs of the same kind, so the sequence is never all-magnetic-then-all-non-magnetic). Whether an object clings is driven solely by its 'magnetic' flag.

name.description: Display name shown under the object and in the trays/results table.

magnetic.description: true = the object leaps up and clings to the magnet; false = it only wobbles and is sorted as non-magnetic.

iconKey.description: Which built-in hand-drawn artwork to render. Must be one of the available iconKeys; unknown keys fall back to a plain gray circle.

quick_round.title: Will it stick?
quick_round.instruction: Drag the magnet onto each object to test it.

kitchen_drawer_theme.title: Test the Kitchen Drawer
kitchen_drawer_theme.subtitle: Which kitchen things does the magnet pick up?

grade_8_restrained.title: Magnetic Materials Test
grade_8_restrained.instruction: Test each object with the magnet and record whether it is attracted.

iconKeys.description: Built-in hand-drawn SVG artwork keys available for object.iconKey. No external images are needed.
iconKeys.note: iconKey only chooses the picture; the magnetic flag alone decides behaviour. Pair an iconKey with whatever name/material/magnetic values you need.

additionalProps.description: The prop signature accepts an additionalProps object for forward-compatibility, but the current component does NOT read it. All dynamic content for this tool is delivered through 'data' (and the top-level 'gradeLevel'). Documented here for schema conformance; to add an additionalProps-driven channel the component would need to be extended.

game.description: Single self-contained sorting game. This tool has no mode selector, prev/next navigation, play/pause, or numbered step sequence, so showModeSelector / showNavigation / showPlayPause / showStepIndicator / initialMode / filterSteps / autoPlayDuration do not apply.

gameFlow.description: The game is continuous rather than a sequence of numbered steps; its phases are:

test.title: Test an object
test.detail: An object slides in; the student drags or taps the magnet onto it. Magnetic objects leap up and cling to the poles with a spark burst; non-magnetic ones wobble and the magnet backs off.

sort.title: Sort into a tray
sort.detail: The identified object flies into the Magnetic or Non-magnetic tray and the next object appears. A progress bar tracks tested / total.

results.title: Results table
results.detail: After every object is tested, a two-column Magnetic vs Non-magnetic table appears with counts, confetti scaled to gradeLevel, and a replay button that reshuffles and restarts.

behavior.interaction: Drag the single magnet onto an object, or tap an object to auto-test it (mobile/accessibility friendly).
behavior.physics: Fixed and non-negotiable: an object clings if and only if its 'magnetic' flag is true. The student cannot get a 'wrong' answer — the magnet simply reveals the truth.
behavior.ordering: Objects are reshuffled into a well-mixed order at the start of each game (no guessable all-magnetic / all-non-magnetic runs).
behavior.responsive: Adapts from small phones to large desktops; chip and table sizing scale to the measured width and the layout reflows without visible scrollbars.
behavior.reducedMotion: Honors prefers-reduced-motion (animations are disabled when the user requests reduced motion).

designSystem.fontFamily: Poppins (loaded via @import at mount), system-ui fallback
